sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Newcastle United have shown decent form in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, with a knack for late goals (50% of goals scored between 76-90 minutes). Chelsea, meanwhile, have been more inconsistent, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, but they’ve demonstrated attacking intent with big chances created in recent games. Chelsea's higher league position (4th vs 12th) and points (28 vs 22) give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Newcastle United have dominated Chelsea, winning both matches 2-0. Despite Chelsea holding more possession in these games, Newcastle capitalized on big chances and defensive errors, which could boost their confidence at St James' Park.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Newcastle’s Bruno Guimarães (7.6 avg. rating last 5 matches, 2 goals) and Anthony Gordon (7.5 avg. rating, 3 goals) are in form and critical to their attack. Chelsea’s Pedro Neto (5 goals, 3 assists this season) and João Pedro (4 goals, 3 assists) will be threats, especially with Newcastle missing key defenders like Dan Burn and Sven Botman.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Newcastle excel in corners (6.125 per game, 1st in league) and aerial duels (54.5% won, 4th in league), which could challenge Chelsea’s defense. Chelsea, with superior possession stats (often over 60% in recent games), may control the midfield but struggle to convert chances, as seen in head-to-head misses.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Trends
sections.text: Both teams often see matches with over 2.5 goals (8/9 for Newcastle recently) and both teams scoring (8/9 for Newcastle). Head-to-head trends suggest high card counts (over 4.5 in 5/6 matches) and fewer corners (under 10.5 in 5/6 matches), aligning with referee Andy Madley’s average of 3.1 yellows per game.

sections.subtitle: Prediction
sections.text: Despite Newcastle’s recent head-to-head success, Chelsea’s better league standing and attacking options tilt the scales. Sofascore’s model (50% away win) supports this, though Newcastle’s home advantage and missing defenders for Chelsea could make it close. Expect a narrow Chelsea victory with both teams likely to score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at St James' Park saw Newcastle United dominate Chelsea with a commanding 2-0 lead. Newcastle showcased their attacking prowess early on, with both goals coming from Nick Woltemade in the 4th and 20th minutes. The home side looked clinical in front of goal, capitalizing on their opportunities, while Chelsea struggled to create any meaningful chances. Discipline was also an issue for the away team, as they picked up three yellow cards compared to none for Newcastle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Newcastle's attacking dominance
halftimeSections.text: Newcastle United were relentless in their attacking play during the first half. They consistently entered the final third and penetrated the penalty area with ease, creating numerous big chances. Key players like Anthony Gordon and Jacob Murphy were instrumental in setting up opportunities, with Gordon contributing significantly to the attacking threat. Despite missing several big chances, their overall pressure overwhelmed Chelsea's defense, leading to the two early goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Chelsea's defensive struggles
halftimeSections.text: Chelsea found themselves on the back foot for most of the first half, unable to cope with Newcastle's attacking intensity. Their defense was repeatedly breached, with minimal entries into the penalty area and no shots on target to show for their efforts. The away side also struggled in duels, losing a significant percentage both on the ground and in the air. This lack of physical presence and defensive organization allowed Newcastle to dictate the tempo of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung heavily in favor of Newcastle United from the early minutes, with consistent attacking spells that kept Chelsea pinned back. After an initial balanced start, Newcastle took control with a series of sustained attacks, especially around the times of their goals. Chelsea showed brief moments of resurgence later in the half, pushing forward in short bursts, but these efforts lacked the intensity or consistency to trouble Newcastle's defense. The home side regained momentum towards the end of the half, finishing strongly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Newcastle, Nick Woltemade stands out as the man of the half, having scored both goals and posing a constant threat to Chelsea's backline. Anthony Gordon also played a crucial role with his creativity and ability to create big chances. On Chelsea's side, players like Cole Palmer and Pedro Neto will need to step up if they are to turn the game around. Moisés Caicedo showed some promise in midfield with his defensive contributions, but more is needed going forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Newcastle United are likely to maintain their aggressive approach, especially with the home crowd behind them at St James' Park. They will aim to exploit any lingering defensive frailties in Chelsea's setup. Chelsea, on the other hand, must regroup quickly and show more urgency in attack if they hope to claw their way back into the game. Tactical adjustments will be key for the away side, potentially bringing on fresh legs to inject energy. Given the current dynamics, Newcastle appear to have the upper hand, but a single goal could shift the momentum.
